claimText: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) granted Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO) status to the Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC) to oversee Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs).
evidenceExcerpt: RBI granted SRO status to the Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC) to oversee NBFCs.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the specific entity granted SRO status for NBFC oversight.
distractorHooks: SEBI granted the SRO status instead of RBI
FIDC was granted SRO status to oversee commercial banks or cooperative banks
Another entity was granted SRO status for NBFCs

claimText: Under RBI's Omnibus Framework 2024, an eligible Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO) must be established as a not-for-profit company registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013.
evidenceExcerpt: Backed by RBI's Omnibus Framework 2024, an eligible SRO must be set up as a not-for-profit company registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the mandatory legal structure and registration requirements for an SRO under RBI guidelines.
distractorHooks: An SRO can be a for-profit company
An SRO must be registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860
An SRO is a statutory body established by a specific Act of Parliament
